content:
pinging anyone that reacted to the thread so I can make sure anyone that downloaded the old file is aware of the new one.
<@249051967219826690> <@272394963642220545> <@1400733999000977510> <@81102419781492736> <@147364225411579904>
I'll work on it after I get better
note that if you make a branch  with this as the base and work on it it will probably be way easier to handle the changes
Can you help me do that, and explain how and why exactly? have just been working within my own excel sheets this whole time, haven't begun to learn how github works

So, basically github is really good at one thing at its core : calculating the difference between two versions of a file, and the "list" of those differences constitutes a *commit*.
The goal of git is to help organize the work of multiple people on the same set of files, and making sure that all the changes (all the commits) can be used by everyone & organized neatly.
If you have the base files as the base, then it is extremely clear what the **differences** are : they're your work.
If you don't, the differences become your changes + the full lines (including your changes) that had never been worked on before.
This means that checking the work done becomes way harder ("*ok, so what changed on that line, lemme pull up the gdrive …*") and it makes you unable to very simply reorder modifications ("*alright I'm sending everything but the obtain->gain for arith since I'm not satisfied with it, let me just work on it again*") since moving it would have you rewrite  part of the next commit.

If you start working and publish some changes to your branch, someone can fork from here and start working on something else.
If you push more changes, then the other person can just grab then as easily as possible as long as you use full files.

I'd be happy to show that in vc with some drawings
This is why I'd recommend you make that excel a csv and just slap it on a branch as a first commit, then everyone that wants to work on gameplay can go from there

> I'd be happy to show that in vc with some drawings
Yes please! I need to get to sleep but I'll try to find time tomorrow. Unsure when exactly I'll have time but when I do I'll hit you up!

I've decided the first major thing I'm going to do is help out by organizing the Gameplay sheet since not a lot of people have bothered to touch it yet, and a lot of languages are missing data entirely. I'll have multiple sheets I'm putting together made from random chunks of rows of the Gameplay source sheet.
